what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG calendar icon using an outlined calendar body, header divider, binding marks, and three filled date dots.
observations: SVG root uses a 24x24 viewBox with explicit width and height of 24.
The icon inherits color through currentColor for stroke and filled dots.
The calendar body is a rounded rectangle positioned from x=3 y=6 to width=18 height=15.
A single path draws the horizontal divider and two top binding marks.
Three small filled circles appear in the lower date area at x positions 8, 12, and 16.
standouts: Uses outline geometry for the calendar shell with filled dot accents for dates.
The shape is compact and self-contained with no external references.
conventions: Uses fill="none" on the SVG root with stroke-based icon styling.
Uses round line caps and joins with stroke-width 1.5.
Uses currentColor so the icon can inherit text or component color.
Breaks from pure stroke-only styling for the date dots by setting fill="currentColor" and stroke="none" on circles.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present, so standalone accessibility semantics are not encoded in the SVG.
notes: A reusable calendar pictogram built as a small currentColor outline SVG with date-dot detail.
questions: Should this icon include accessible labeling when used standalone?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a calendar icon, and the SVG content draws a calendar icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable calendar symbol that adapts to surrounding color while staying visually lightweight.
contribution: Adds a compact currentColor calendar glyph with outline structure and filled date markers for use in a fused visual asset system.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG calendar icon with a rounded outline body, divider line, binding marks, and three filled date dots.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to serve as a reusable UI calendar icon that scales cleanly and inherits component color.
